subject: Statistics
title: Instrumental Variables
description: A fast breakdown of Instrumental Variables for builders.

hook.text: Does education increase earnings? People who get more education differ in motivation. You can't just compare them.

buildup.text: An instrumental variable affects education but doesn't directly affect earnings — like distance to the nearest college.

discovery.text: People born near a college get more education due to convenience. The extra education's effect on earnings is causal.

twist.text: Good instruments are rare. The variable must affect treatment but not outcome. This is hard to guarantee.

climax.text: Angrist and Imbens won the 2021 Nobel Prize partly for their work on instrumental variable methods.

punchline.text: Find the right lever. It moves treatment without touching the outcome.

quiz.question: What makes a good instrumental variable?
options: It affects the treatment but does not directly affect the outcome
It directly predicts both treatment and outcome
It must be a random number
